verbatim_text: That's what we just saw. But can they formally parametrize a given algorithm? And here I'll talk about one of my works, which kind of says that. And the simple idea here, it's sort of a proof by picture. So if you take a tokenized problem as input to this LLM, it decodes a block of tokens. It decodes it autoregressively. Let's denote a block of tokens as operation. It's operation O1. You feed this back into the LLM. It decodes another block of tokens, operation O2. O2 gives O3, and so on, until you get to the final. Now, many works, including our own, have tried to analyze these operations, and I found that broadly these operations constitute a form of search or an in-context search. At least they verbalize a form of in-context search. You can correspond this first operation, O1, with some particular skill, like generating a particular attempt on this problem.
